verses.text: The lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, once darkened, shall see a great light; 'the people that walked in darkness have seen a great light.' Hope dawns for Galilee. This light is Christ, whose coming there fulfilled the promise.

The lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, once darkened, shall see a great light; 'the people that walked in darkness have seen a great light.' Hope dawns for Galilee. This light is Christ, whose coming there fulfilled the promise.

God multiplies the nation and its joy as in harvest, breaking the yoke and the oppressor's rod 'as in the day of Midian.' Burdens are lifted. The Deliverer brings release like Gideon's victory of old.

God multiplies the nation and its joy as in harvest, breaking the yoke and the oppressor's rod 'as in the day of Midian.' Burdens are lifted. The Deliverer brings release like Gideon's victory of old.

'For unto us a child is born... and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace,' His government and peace without end. This is Christ, the promised King. The eternal Son rules a kingdom that shall never fail.

'For unto us a child is born... and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace,' His government and peace without end. This is Christ, the promised King. The eternal Son rules a kingdom that shall never fail.

God sends His word against proud Jacob, who say in arrogance they will rebuild stronger; so He stirs up adversaries to devour them. Pride refuses correction. Those who will not humble themselves invite worse blows.

God sends His word against proud Jacob, who say in arrogance they will rebuild stronger; so He stirs up adversaries to devour them. Pride refuses correction. Those who will not humble themselves invite worse blows.

Still the people turn not to Him that smites them, so He cuts off head and tail in one day; even the fatherless find no mercy in such an age. Hardened hearts feel the rod in vain. Refused repentance brings sweeping judgment.

Wickedness burns like fire, devouring the people as fuel, brother turning on brother; 'for all this his anger is not turned away.' Sin consumes a society from within. The refrain warns that judgment is not yet finished.
